emotion_shift.input:
Agent context: Daniel handling billing dispute.
Customer text: I am thrilled this happened again, amazing service.
Acoustic emotion: anger

Task: Detect cross-modal contradiction and classify type.
emotion_shift.reference_answer: Cross-modal contradiction flagged. Customer uses positive lexical sentiment ('thrilled', 'amazing') while acoustic emotion is anger. Type: Sarcasm.
emotion_shift.scoring_criteria: Must identify sarcasm, cite 'thrilled' or 'amazing', note anger vs positive text mismatch

emotion_shift.input:
Agent context: Priya handling outage credit call.
Customer text: Okay, do whatever you want.
Acoustic emotion: disgust

Task: Detect cross-modal contradiction and classify type.
emotion_shift.reference_answer: Passive-aggression detected. Resignation language ('do whatever you want') with hostile disgust prosody. Cross-modal contradiction flagged.
emotion_shift.scoring_criteria: Must identify passive-aggression, cite 'do whatever you want', note disgust acoustic mismatch

emotion_shift.input:
Transcript chunk (8 turns):
Agent: I already told you that we need your account number before I can look this up.
Customer: You keep asking me the same thing. I gave it to you twice.
Agent: Well, if you'd listened the first time we wouldn't be here.
Customer: That's not helpful.
Acoustic note: agent turn 3 delivered with neutral tone but curt wording; customer acoustic frustration rising.

Task: Detect emotion shift / cross-modal signals.
emotion_shift.reference_answer: Agent showed passive-aggression in turn 3 ('Well, if you'd listened the first time') contradicting neutral acoustic tone. Cross-modal contradiction flagged.
emotion_shift.scoring_criteria: Must identify passive-aggression, cite turn 3 quote, note cross-modal mismatch

emotion_shift.input:
Transcript chunk:
Customer: My internet was out for two days last week. I work from home, so that wasn't great. I want a credit on my bill.
Acoustic emotion: frustrated
Text emotion: frustrated

Task: Detect cross-modal contradiction.
emotion_shift.reference_answer: No cross-modal contradiction. Text and acoustic emotions align (genuine frustration). is_dissonance_detected: false.
emotion_shift.scoring_criteria: Must return true negative — no sarcasm/passive-aggression; aligned frustration

emotion_shift.input:
Transcript chunk (CALL_11):
Customer: I'm really worried — there's an eighty-seven dollar charge I absolutely did not make.
Agent: Yeah, I hear you. Let's just get through the verification first.
Acoustic note: agent delivery impatient and dismissive while words are superficially acknowledging.

Task: Detect cross-modal contradiction.
emotion_shift.reference_answer: Cross-modal contradiction: agent words acknowledge worry but acoustic delivery is dismissive/impatient. Type: Passive-Aggression or tone mismatch.
emotion_shift.scoring_criteria: Must flag tone mismatch between empathetic words and dismissive delivery; cite customer worry quote

process_adherence.input:
Topic hint: refund_request
Transcript:
Customer: I want a refund on my last invoice for the outage.
Agent: Sure, I can help. What's your account number?
Customer: 8847-2231-09.
Agent: Thanks. I'll process a full refund to your card right now.

Retrieved SOP: [SOP-01 Refund Request Processing]
Expected resolution graph steps:
- Acknowledge customer issue
- Collect order identifier
- Verify refund eligibility window
- Confirm refund method and timeline
- Close with summary and next steps
process_adherence.reference_answer: Missing SOP steps: [Verify refund eligibility window, Confirm refund method and timeline, Close with summary and next steps]. Steps completed: [Acknowledge customer issue, Collect order identifier].
process_adherence.scoring_criteria: Must list missing eligibility verification and refund method/timeline confirmation

process_adherence.input:
Topic hint: billing_issue
Transcript:
Customer: My bill is thirty dollars higher than last month.
Agent: I see a new equipment rental fee. That explains the increase.
Customer: Nobody told me about that.
Agent: It's on page two of your bill.

Expected resolution graph steps:
- Acknowledge billing concern
- Verify account and charge details
- Explain charge source or correction
- Confirm customer understanding
- Close with follow-up path
process_adherence.reference_answer: Missing SOP steps: [Confirm customer understanding, Close with follow-up path]. Agent explained charge but did not confirm understanding or offer follow-up.
process_adherence.scoring_criteria: Must list missing confirm understanding and close steps

process_adherence.input:
Topic hint: technical_support
Transcript:
Customer: My internet is really slow tonight.
Agent: Have you tried rebooting the router?
Customer: Yes, twice.
Agent: Unplug it for thirty seconds and plug it back in.
Customer: I already did that.

Expected resolution graph steps:
- Acknowledge the technical issue
- Collect device or account context
- Run step-by-step troubleshooting
- Validate issue resolution
- Document next escalation path
process_adherence.reference_answer: Missing SOP steps: [Collect device or account context, Validate issue resolution, Document next escalation path]. Agent repeated reboot without collecting context or escalating.
process_adherence.scoring_criteria: Must identify missing context collection and escalation documentation

process_adherence.input:
Topic hint: account_access
Transcript:
Customer: I can't log into my account after the password reset email.
Agent: I'll send another reset link now.
Customer: Got it, I'm in. Thank you.
Agent: Great, you're all set.

Expected resolution graph steps:
- Acknowledge access issue
- Verify user identity
- Guide reset or unlock steps
- Confirm successful login
- Close with prevention advice
process_adherence.reference_answer: Missing SOP steps: [Verify user identity, Close with prevention advice]. Completed: acknowledge, guide reset, confirm login.
process_adherence.scoring_criteria: Must list missing identity verification and prevention advice

process_adherence.input:
Topic hint: refund_request
Transcript (CALL_01 — complete adherence):
Customer: Hi, our internet was down for almost two days last week and I need a credit on my bill.
Agent: I'm sorry about the outage. I can help with a credit. May I have your account number?
Customer: Sure, it's 8847-2231-09.
Agent: Thank you. For security, please confirm the last four on your payment method and your account PIN.
Customer: Last four is 4421, PIN is 7392.
Agent: I've confirmed a 44-hour outage in our NOC log. Your pro-rated credit is twenty-three dollars and thirty-three cents on your next bill.
Customer: That works, thanks.
Agent: I've applied the credit. Your case reference is NX-2026-0142. You'll see it on your next PDF bill within five business days.

Expected resolution graph steps:
- Acknowledge customer issue
- Collect order identifier
- Verify refund eligibility window
- Confirm refund method and timeline
- Close with summary and next steps
process_adherence.reference_answer: No missing SOP steps. Complete adherence: all five resolution graph steps evidenced in transcript.
process_adherence.scoring_criteria: True negative — must report complete adherence with empty or no missing steps list

process_adherence.input:
Topic hint: retention
Transcript:
Customer: I'm thinking about canceling and going to FiberWave.
Agent: I understand. What plan are you on today?
Customer: Premium 500 meg.
Agent: We can match their price if you stay.
Customer: Maybe. I'll think about it.
Agent: Okay, call us back anytime.

Expected resolution graph steps:
- Acknowledge cancellation intent
- Identify reason for leaving
- Offer retention incentive
- Confirm decision or schedule follow-up
- Close with summary
process_adherence.reference_answer: Missing SOP steps: [Identify reason for leaving, Confirm decision or schedule follow-up, Close with summary]. Agent offered match but did not probe reason or confirm follow-up.
process_adherence.scoring_criteria: Must list missing reason probe and follow-up/close steps

process_adherence.input:
Topic hint: fraud_dispute

Agent: I'll open a fraud ticket and freeze that amount.

Expected resolution graph steps:
- Acknowledge fraud concern
- Verify customer identity
- Document disputed charge
- Explain investigation timeline
- Advise password change and monitoring

process_adherence.input:

Customer: Can you waive the late fee on my account? I paid one day late because of travel.
Agent: I see a fifteen dollar late fee on March 12.

- Verify account and fee details

process_adherence.input:
Topic hint: technical_support
Transcript (complete adherence):
Customer: My router keeps dropping connection every hour.
Agent: I'm sorry you're dealing with that. What's your account number and router model?
Customer: Account 5521-0098-44, it's the NexaLink AC3200.
Agent: Thanks. Let's reboot the router and check signal levels. Unplug power for thirty seconds, then plug back in.
Customer: Done. It's back online.
Agent: Great. I'll monitor your line for twenty-four hours. If drops continue, I'll escalate to tier-two with ticket TS-8821.
Customer: Thank you.
Agent: You're welcome. Reference TS-8821 if it happens again.

Expected resolution graph steps:
- Acknowledge the technical issue
- Collect device or account context
- Run step-by-step troubleshooting
- Validate issue resolution
- Document next escalation path
process_adherence.reference_answer: No missing SOP steps. Complete adherence: all five steps evidenced including escalation path TS-8821.
process_adherence.scoring_criteria: True negative — complete adherence

nli_policy.input:
Ground truth policy:
Refunds for service outages are processed within 5–7 business days as bill credits, not card refunds.

Agent statement:
I'll push the refund to your card within 72 hours.
nli_policy.reference_answer: Verdict: Contradiction. Agent promised 72-hour card refund; policy states 5–7 business days as bill credits.
nli_policy.scoring_criteria: Must return Contradiction label and cite the 72-hour vs 5-7 days discrepancy

nli_policy.input:
Ground truth policy:
Outages under 24 hours are not eligible for automatic service credits.

Agent statement:
Your six-hour outage doesn't meet the 24-hour threshold, so no automatic credit applies under policy.
nli_policy.reference_answer: Verdict: Entailment. Agent correctly states the 24-hour eligibility threshold from policy.
nli_policy.scoring_criteria: Must return Entailment and reference 24-hour threshold

nli_policy._note: Reference corrected 2026-06-13: agent requests only 2 of 5 verification factors before adjustment — violates 3-of-5 policy; all models scored Contradiction in original run.
nli_policy.input:
Ground truth policy:
Agents must complete 3-of-5 identity verification before any financial adjustment.

Agent statement:
I completely understand how stressful unauthorized charges can be. Before we adjust anything, I need to verify your account number and PIN.
nli_policy.reference_answer: Verdict: Contradiction. Agent proposes only two verification factors (account number and PIN) before a financial adjustment; policy requires 3-of-5.
nli_policy.scoring_criteria: Must return Contradiction — insufficient verification count vs 3-of-5 rule

nli_policy.input:
Ground truth policy:
Frontline agents may apply goodwill credits up to $200 without manager approval.

Agent statement:
Our policy requires manager approval for any goodwill credit and charges a $25 processing fee.
nli_policy.reference_answer: Verdict: Policy Hallucination. Agent invented manager-approval requirement and processing fee not present in policy.
nli_policy.scoring_criteria: Must return Policy Hallucination for fabricated fee and approval rule

nli_policy.input:
Ground truth policy (CALL_10):
Outage credit threshold is 24 hours. Agent cap for goodwill credits is $200. Refund timeline must use approved script FIN-RULE-010.

Agent statement:
The threshold is 12 hours and I can approve up to $150, and you'll see the refund in 3 days.
nli_policy.reference_answer: Verdict: Contradiction / Policy Hallucination. Agent misquoted 12-hour threshold (actual 24h), $150 cap (actual $200), and fabricated timeline.
nli_policy.scoring_criteria: Must flag wrong threshold and cap figures vs policy

nli_policy.input:
Ground truth policy:
Agents must read the approved recording notice within the first 15 seconds of the call.

Agent statement:
This call may be recorded for quality and training purposes.
nli_policy.reference_answer: Verdict: Entailment. Agent delivers the required recording notice consistent with policy.
nli_policy.scoring_criteria: Must return Entailment for compliant recording notice

nli_policy.input:
Ground truth policy:
Goodwill credits may be applied up to $200 without manager approval.

Agent statement:
I can apply a one-time goodwill credit of up to two hundred dollars without escalating to a manager.
nli_policy.reference_answer: Verdict: Entailment. Agent correctly states the $200 frontline cap.
nli_policy.scoring_criteria: Must return Entailment and cite $200 cap

nli_policy.input:
Ground truth policy:
Outage credits are applied as bill credits on the next invoice, not as card refunds.

Agent statement:
Thanks for calling NexaLink today. How can I help?
nli_policy.reference_answer: Verdict: Benign Deviation. Greeting/small talk not specified in policy and does not contradict the credit rule.
nli_policy.scoring_criteria: Must return Benign Deviation for neutral greeting

nli_policy.input:
Ground truth policy:
Agents must not promise exact repair ETAs unless confirmed in the NOC ticket system.

Agent statement:
Our engineers will definitely have your service restored by 3 PM today — I just know it.
nli_policy.reference_answer: Verdict: Policy Hallucination / Contradiction. Agent guarantees a specific ETA without NOC confirmation, violating the policy constraint.

nli_policy.input:
Ground truth policy:
Late fees may be waived once per rolling 12-month period for accounts in good standing.

Agent statement:
I've waived your late fee. This is the second waiver we've applied this year on your account.
nli_policy.reference_answer: Verdict: Contradiction. Agent describes a second waiver within 12 months, violating the once-per-period rule.
nli_policy.scoring_criteria: Must return Contradiction for second waiver in period

rag_judge.input:
--- COMPANY POLICIES ---
[FIN-RULE-001 | Refund Policy > Outage Credits]
Outages of 24+ hours qualify for pro-rated credits applied to the next bill.

--- AGENT TRANSCRIPT ---
Agent verified identity, confirmed 44-hour outage in NOC log, applied $23.33 credit, explained bill-credit timeline.
rag_judge.reference_answer: Compliant. compliance_score >= 0.8. Source: FIN-RULE-001 outage credit policy. No violations.
rag_judge.scoring_criteria: Verdict must be compliant/high score; reference FIN-RULE-001

rag_judge.input:
--- COMPANY POLICIES ---
[CS-RULE-008 | Communication Standards]
Agents must not talk over the customer or use dismissive tone.

--- AGENT TRANSCRIPT ---
Agent interrupted customer twice, used curt responses ('Yeah, I hear you'), and matched customer's rising frustration instead of de-escalating.
rag_judge.reference_answer: Non-compliant. Violations: talked over customer (CS-RULE-008), dismissive tone, failed de-escalation.
rag_judge.scoring_criteria: Verdict non-compliant; cite CS-RULE-008 communication violation

rag_judge.input:

--- AGENT TRANSCRIPT ---

Agent on call NX-000018: skipped a required FIN-RULE-001 step and proceeded without citing Refund Policy > Outage Credits.

[FIN-RULE-010 | Refund Timeline Script]
Credits appear on the next PDF bill within 5 business days.

--- AGENT TRANSCRIPT ---
Agent on call NX-000019: followed FIN-RULE-010 — stated required script and cited Refund Timeline Script correctly before proceeding.

[CS-RULE-001 | Greeting]
Agents must use the approved NexaLink greeting verbatim.

Agent on call NX-000020: followed CS-RULE-001 — stated required script and cited Greeting correctly before proceeding.

text_to_sql.input:
Organization ID: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001
Question: Show me the 5 agents with the lowest average compliance score this month
text_to_sql.reference_answer: SELECT u.name, ROUND(AVG(pc.compliance_score)::NUMERIC * 10, 1) AS avg_score FROM users u JOIN interactions i ON i.agent_id = u.id AND i.organization_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001' JOIN policy_compliance pc ON pc.interaction_id = i.id WHERE u.role = 'agent' AND i.interaction_date >= date_trunc('month', now()) GROUP BY u.id, u.name ORDER BY avg_score ASC LIMIT 5
text_to_sql.scoring_criteria: SELECT only; correct tables: users, interactions, policy_compliance; GROUP BY and ORDER BY ASC LIMIT 5

text_to_sql.input:
Organization ID: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001
Question: Who are the top 5 agents by overall score?
text_to_sql.reference_answer: SELECT u.name, ROUND(AVG(s.overall_score)::NUMERIC * 10, 1) AS avg_score FROM users u JOIN interactions i ON i.agent_id = u.id AND i.organization_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001' JOIN interaction_scores s ON s.interaction_id = i.id WHERE u.role = 'agent' GROUP BY u.id, u.name ORDER BY avg_score DESC LIMIT 5
text_to_sql.scoring_criteria: SELECT only; users + interactions + interaction_scores; ORDER BY DESC LIMIT 5

text_to_sql.input:
Organization ID: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001
Question: How many calls were not resolved in the last 30 days?
text_to_sql.reference_answer: SELECT COUNT(*) AS unresolved_count FROM interactions i JOIN interaction_scores s ON s.interaction_id = i.id WHERE i.organization_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001' AND s.was_resolved = false AND i.interaction_date >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'
text_to_sql.scoring_criteria: COUNT with was_resolved=false and 30-day date filter

text_to_sql.input:
Organization ID: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001
Question: What are the most common customer emotions?
text_to_sql.reference_answer: SELECT u2.emotion, COUNT(*) AS count FROM utterances u2 JOIN interactions i ON u2.interaction_id = i.id WHERE u2.speaker_role = 'customer' AND i.organization_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001' GROUP BY u2.emotion ORDER BY count DESC LIMIT 10
text_to_sql.scoring_criteria: utterances filtered by speaker_role=customer with emotion GROUP BY (per infra/db/01_schema.sql)

text_to_sql.input:
Organization ID: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001
Question: List all policy violations this week
text_to_sql.reference_answer: SELECT i.id::text AS interaction_id, cp.policy_title, ROUND(pc.compliance_score::NUMERIC * 10, 1) AS compliance_score FROM policy_compliance pc JOIN company_policies cp ON pc.policy_id = cp.id JOIN interactions i ON pc.interaction_id = i.id WHERE pc.is_compliant = false AND i.organization_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001' AND i.interaction_date >= date_trunc('week', now()) LIMIT 50

text_to_sql.input:
Organization ID: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001
Question: Agents with most frustrated customer utterances (SQL-006)
text_to_sql.reference_answer: SELECT u.name, COUNT(*) AS frustrated_count FROM users u JOIN interactions i ON i.agent_id = u.id JOIN utterances ut ON ut.interaction_id = i.id WHERE i.organization_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001' AND ut.speaker_role = 'customer' AND ut.emotion = 'frustrated' GROUP BY u.id, u.name ORDER BY frustrated_count DESC LIMIT 9

text_to_sql.input:
Organization ID: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001
Question: Daily call volume last 14 days (SQL-007)
text_to_sql.reference_answer: SELECT DATE(i.interaction_date) AS day, COUNT(*) AS calls FROM interactions i WHERE i.organization_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001' AND i.interaction_date >= NOW() - INTERVAL '14 days' GROUP BY day ORDER BY day

text_to_sql.input:
Organization ID: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001
Question: Resolved rate by agent this month (SQL-008)
text_to_sql.reference_answer: SELECT u.name, ROUND(100.0 * AVG(CASE WHEN s.was_resolved THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 1) AS pct FROM users u JOIN interactions i ON i.agent_id = u.id JOIN interaction_scores s ON s.interaction_id = i.id WHERE i.organization_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001' AND u.role = 'agent' AND i.interaction_date >= date_trunc('month', now()) GROUP BY u.id, u.name ORDER BY pct DESC

text_to_sql.input:
Organization ID: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001
Question: Longest calls over 309 seconds this month (SQL-009)
text_to_sql.reference_answer: SELECT i.id, i.duration_seconds, u.name FROM interactions i JOIN users u ON u.id = i.agent_id WHERE i.organization_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001' AND i.duration_seconds > 309 AND i.interaction_date >= date_trunc('month', now()) ORDER BY i.duration_seconds DESC LIMIT 4

text_to_sql.input:
Organization ID: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001
Question: Top 5 agents by overall score in the last 17 days (SQL-010)
text_to_sql.reference_answer: SELECT u.name, ROUND(AVG(s.overall_score)::NUMERIC * 10, 1) AS avg_score FROM users u JOIN interactions i ON i.agent_id = u.id AND i.organization_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001' JOIN interaction_scores s ON s.interaction_id = i.id WHERE u.role = 'agent' AND i.interaction_date >= NOW() - INTERVAL '17 days' GROUP BY u.id, u.name ORDER BY avg_score DESC LIMIT 5

Question: How many unresolved calls in the last 18 days? (SQL-011)

text_to_sql.input:
Organization ID: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001
Question: Most common customer emotions this month (SQL-012)
text_to_sql.reference_answer: SELECT u2.emotion, COUNT(*) AS count FROM utterances u2 JOIN interactions i ON u2.interaction_id = i.id WHERE u2.speaker_role = 'customer' AND i.organization_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001' AND i.interaction_date >= date_trunc('month', now()) GROUP BY u2.emotion ORDER BY count DESC LIMIT 10

text_to_sql.input:

Question: Average handle time for human agents (SQL-013)
text_to_sql.reference_answer: SELECT ROUND(AVG(i.duration_seconds)::NUMERIC, 1) AS avg_aht FROM interactions i JOIN users u ON u.id = i.agent_id WHERE i.organization_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001' AND u.role = 'agent' AND u.agent_type = 'human'

text_to_sql.input:

Question: Interactions with empathy score below 0.5 this week (SQL-014)

Question: Longest calls over 319 seconds this month (SQL-019)
